title: VCD dump from command line
body:
Hi!
I did look at the command line reference, and AFAICT there is no way to enable a VCD dump directly from the command line, am I correct?

Would it be reasonable to have such an option?
For example, setting the initial path of the dump file, which can then be of course  overriden with a Verilog $dumpfile(), if any.

The reason I am wondering is, I am developing and using [PyXHDL](https://github.com/davidel/pyxhdl), which is a Python front-end to SystemVerilog and VHDL, and the latter has no equivalent of $dumpfile/$dumpvars.

This is not an issue, since things like GHDL (which is the simulator I use for VHDL, together with Verilator for Verilog) have command line options to enable a VCD dump.

Having such feature would allow me to delegate the VCD dump handling to the command line, compared to having a Python level dumpfile() API, which in VHDL would do nothing (with the user expecting a VCD dump).
